response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced polyglot engineer with a strong background in TypeScript, Node.js, and developer tooling, but he is fundamentally misaligned with the core requirements of this role. The position demands deep Java and Kotlin expertise, JVM internals knowledge, and hands-on experience with Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate — none of which are present in any meaningful way on this resume. His Java exposure is limited to a brief period at Altom (2016–2018) in a non-JVM-focused context, and there is no evidence of IDE plugin development, IntelliJ Platform work, or enterprise Java framework experience. His developer tooling instincts (NI LabWindows, MCP generators, CI infrastructure) are a faint positive signal, but insufficient to bridge the technical gap.
strengths: Genuine developer-tooling orientation: contributed to a C IDE at National Instruments, built test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and currently works on MCP code generators and agentic developer infrastructure
Demonstrated ability to operate at scale: handled millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby using low-latency, high-performance systems design
Broad software craftsmanship practices: TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, code katas, and public workshops show engineering discipline beyond just shipping features
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin expertise — the role's primary requirement; Java appears only incidentally in two roles from 2016–2018 and is listed last in skills
Zero Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience anywhere on the resume — the entire enterprise framework domain the role centers on is absent
No JVM tooling, IDE plugin development, or IntelliJ Platform experience; the NI LabWindows stint was a C IDE and does not transfer to JVM IDE internals
